column8: People who travel into space are called "astronauts" in English. If they come from Russian-speaking countries, they are also_____________as "cosmonauts". (KNOW)
column9: Mane astronauts and cosmonauts_____________into space since space travel began, over forty years ago. (FLY)
column10: People have been to the Moon, and might_______________the planet Mars quite soon. (VISIT)
column11: However, before Yuri Gagarin made his historic flight in 1961, no human_____________outside the Earth's atmosphere. (TRAVEL)
column12: His flight was one of the___________moments in the history of the world. (GREAT)
column13: Two years later, on 16th June 1963, Valentina Tereshkova, also from Russia, became the___________woman to go into space. (ONE)
column14: She took just over 70 hours to go round the Earth 48 times, before___________successfully back in Russia.
column15: Valentina Tereshkova never___________into space again, but she later received the United Nations Gold Medal of Peace. (GO)
